title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: c14_calibrate() calibrates conventional radiocarbon ages errors calendar timescale: default, c14_calibrate() uses IntCal20 calibration curve (Reimer et al. 2020), recommended terrestrial dates northern hemisphere. can change c14_curve argument (see ?IntCal20 available curves). c14_calibrate() returns year era (era::yr vector), indicating age expressed calibrated years Present (‘cal BP’). functions package return yrs work internally ensure calendric calculations consistent. Calibration vectorised, can calibrate many dates : unlocks real utility c14 package, embedding calibration tidy workflow. example, shub1_c14 dataset (provided package) contains 27 radiocarbon dates Epipalaeolithic site Shubayqa 1 eastern Jordan. can calibrate dates within data frame using dplyr’s mutate() verb: cal column preserves original data alongside calibrated dates, can continue working full dataset.
code: c14_calibrate(5000, 30) #> <c14_cal[1]> #> [1] 5604–5891 cal BP ages <- c(5000, 6000, 7000) errors <- c(30, 40, 50)  c14_calibrate(ages, errors) #> <c14_cal[3]> #> [1] 5604–5891 cal BP 6740–6946 cal BP 7705–7934 cal BP shub1_c14 |>   mutate(cal = c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error)) #> # A tibble: 27 × 9 #>    lab_id      context phase   sample         material c14_age c14_error outlier #>    <chr>         <int> <chr>   <chr>          <chr>      <int>     <int> <lgl>   #>  1 RTD-7951         23 Phase 7 Context 126, … Bolbosc…   12166        55 FALSE   #>  2 Beta-112146      24 Phase 7 SHUB1/105      gazelle…   12310        60 FALSE   #>  3 RTD-7317         26 Phase 7 Context 83, S… Bolbosc…   12289        46 FALSE   #>  4 RTD-7318         27 Phase 7 Context 86, S… Zilla s…   12332        46 FALSE   #>  5 RTD-7948         24 Phase 7 Context 120, … Bolbosc…   12478        38 FALSE   #>  6 RTD-7947         22 Phase 6 Context 166, … Bolbosc…   12322        38 FALSE   #>  7 RTD-7313         22 Phase 6 Context 67, K… Bolbosc…   12346        46 FALSE   #>  8 RTD-7311         22 Phase 6 Context 67, K… Vitex s…   12367        65 FALSE   #>  9 RTD-7312         22 Phase 6 Context 67, K… Zilla s…   12405        50 FALSE   #> 10 RTD-7314         22 Phase 6 Context 67, K… Bolbosc…   12273        48 FALSE   #> # ℹ 17 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: cal <cal>

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: table last example showed highest density interval calibrated radiocarbon date (95.4% probability – sometimes called ‘2-sigma’ range) calibrated radiocarbon date, rather full probability distribution. 1 can calculate explicitly cal_hdi(), also allows us choose another probability value: cal_hdr() always returns single region probability distribution – shortest interval containing specified probability mass. Calibrated radiocarbon dates often multimodal, can accurate consider one highest density regions, can calculate cal_hdr(): inverse two functions cal_probability(), calculates probability calibrated date falls within specified time interval: functions summarising calibrated radiocarbon dates include various methods calculating point estimate (see ?cal_point). Generally speaking, ‘good’ point estimate calibrated radiocarbon distribution (Michczyński 2007), can nevertheless useful working range full probability distribution option. case, mode reasonable choice: Note mean summarise sense deriving summary statistics calendar probability distribution. return vector length input therefore dplyr terms mutating functions, summarising functions; operations can used dplyr::summarise() covered section aggregating calibrated dates.
code: shub1_c14 |>   mutate(     cal = c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error),     cal_hdi_1sigma = cal_hdi(cal, interval = 0.683)   ) |>   select(lab_id, c14_age, c14_error, cal, cal_hdi_1sigma) #> # A tibble: 27 × 5 #>    lab_id      c14_age c14_error                cal cal_hdi_1sigma #>    <chr>         <int>     <int>              <cal> <list>         #>  1 RTD-7951      12166        55 13813–14303 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  2 Beta-112146   12310        60 14074–14821 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  3 RTD-7317      12289        46 14072–14803 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  4 RTD-7318      12332        46 14102–14820 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  5 RTD-7948      12478        38 14348–14980 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  6 RTD-7947      12322        38 14103–14811 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  7 RTD-7313      12346        46 14116–14828 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  8 RTD-7311      12367        65 14128–14850 cal BP <yr [2]>       #>  9 RTD-7312      12405        50 14216–14878 cal BP <yr [2]>       #> 10 RTD-7314      12273        48 14058–14804 cal BP <yr [2]>       #> # ℹ 17 more rows c14_calibrate(8800, 30) |>   cal_hdr() #> [[1]] #> [[1]][[1]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 9681 9920 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #> [[1]][[2]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 9935 9956 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #> [[1]][[3]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1]  9995 10007 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #> [[1]][[4]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 10067 10116 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 x <- c14_calibrate(1116, 30) cal_probability(x, 970, 1060) # cal BP #> [1] 0.8086552 shub1_c14 |>   mutate(     cal = c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error),     cal_mode = cal_mode(cal)   ) |>   select(lab_id, c14_age, c14_error, cal, cal_mode) #> Warning: There were 8 warnings in `mutate()`. #> The first warning was: #> ℹ In argument: `cal_mode = cal_mode(cal)`. #> Caused by warning: #> ! `x` has more than one modal value. Only the first will be returned. #> ℹ Run `dplyr::last_dplyr_warnings()` to see the 7 remaining warnings. #> # A tibble: 27 × 5 #>    lab_id      c14_age c14_error                cal     cal_mode #>    <chr>         <int>     <int>              <cal>         <yr> #>  1 RTD-7951      12166        55 13813–14303 cal BP 14065 cal BP #>  2 Beta-112146   12310        60 14074–14821 cal BP 14205 cal BP #>  3 RTD-7317      12289        46 14072–14803 cal BP 14185 cal BP #>  4 RTD-7318      12332        46 14102–14820 cal BP 14250 cal BP #>  5 RTD-7948      12478        38 14348–14980 cal BP 14640 cal BP #>  6 RTD-7947      12322        38 14103–14811 cal BP 14245 cal BP #>  7 RTD-7313      12346        46 14116–14828 cal BP 14255 cal BP #>  8 RTD-7311      12367        65 14128–14850 cal BP 14340 cal BP #>  9 RTD-7312      12405        50 14216–14878 cal BP 14375 cal BP #> 10 RTD-7314      12273        48 14058–14804 cal BP 14165 cal BP #> # ℹ 17 more rows

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: package provides default plot() method calibrated dates shows probability distributions calibration curve.  default method plots calibrated dates passed . 2 can arrange multiple plots , example, par(mfrow):
code: shub1_cal <- c14_calibrate(shub1_c14$c14_age, shub1_c14$c14_error) plot(shub1_cal[1]) par(mfrow = c(2, 1)) plot(shub1_cal[1:2]) par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: c14 works standard R data structures, straightforward build custom ggplots ggplot2. key use cal_dist() extract probability distributions, tidyr::unnest() tidy data frame. cal_dist() returns list data frames, age pdens columns. stored tibble list-column, unnest() expands element rows:  can plot multiple dates faceting. original data columns (like lab_id) preserved can used directly:  makes easy compare dates across sites, phases, grouping data.
code: shub1_c14 |>   slice(1) |>   mutate(cal = c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error)) |>   mutate(dist = cal_dist(cal)) |>   unnest(dist) |>   ggplot(aes(x = age, y = pdens)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = pdens)) +   labs(x = "Calendar age (cal BP)", y = "Probability density") +   scale_x_reverse() shub1_c14 |>   slice(1:6) |>   mutate(cal = c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error)) |>   mutate(dist = cal_dist(cal)) |>   unnest(dist) |>   ggplot(aes(x = age, y = pdens)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = pdens)) +   facet_wrap(vars(lab_id), ncol = 1, scales = "free_y") +   labs(x = "Calendar age (cal BP)", y = "Probability density") +   scale_x_reverse()

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: c14 provides two methods combining multiple calibrated dates single summary distribution.

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: cal_sum() adds probability distributions dates vector. simplest aggregation method.  can also use sum() method:
code: shub1_cal <- c14_calibrate(shub1_c14$c14_age, shub1_c14$c14_error)  summed <- cal_sum(shub1_cal) sum_df <- vctrs::vec_data(summed)[[1]]  ggplot(sum_df, aes(x = age, y = pdens)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = pdens), fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred") +   labs(x = "Calendar age (cal BP)", y = "Summed probability") +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal() sum(shub1_cal) #> <c14_cal_dist[1]> #> [1] c. 14250 cal BP

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: cal_density() uses composite kernel density estimation (cKDE) produce smoother summary distribution. resamples date’s probability distribution fits kernel density estimate, optional bootstrapping estimate uncertainty.  bootstrapped standard error (.error) gives measure uncertainty density estimate.
code: density_est <- cal_density(shub1_cal, bw = 30, times = 10) density_est #> # A tibble: 14,792 × 3 #>           age .estimate   .error #>          <yr>     <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 789 cal BP  9.32e-21 2.09e-20 #>  2 790 cal BP  9.25e-21 2.05e-20 #>  3 791 cal BP  9.18e-21 2.01e-20 #>  4 792 cal BP  9.11e-21 1.96e-20 #>  5 793 cal BP  9.04e-21 1.92e-20 #>  6 794 cal BP  8.97e-21 1.87e-20 #>  7 795 cal BP  8.89e-21 1.83e-20 #>  8 796 cal BP  8.82e-21 1.78e-20 #>  9 797 cal BP  8.75e-21 1.74e-20 #> 10 798 cal BP  8.68e-21 1.70e-20 #> # ℹ 14,782 more rows ggplot(density_est, aes(x = age, y = .estimate)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = .estimate - .error, ymax = .estimate + .error),               fill = "darkred", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "darkred") +   labs(x = "Calendar age (cal BP)", y = "Density") +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal()

title: Tidy radiocarbon data
text: methods can stratified grouping variable. useful comparing periods phases. compare groups, run aggregation separately stratum:
code: phases <- unique(shub1_c14$phase)  phase_densities <- purrr::map(phases, function(p) {   idx <- shub1_c14$phase == p   cal_density(shub1_cal[idx], bw = 30, times = 10) |>     mutate(phase = p) }) |> bind_rows()  ggplot(phase_densities, aes(x = age, y = .estimate)) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = .estimate - .error, ymax = .estimate + .error),               fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.3) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue") +   facet_wrap(~phase, ncol = 1) +   labs(x = "Calendar age (cal BP)", y = "Density") +   scale_x_reverse() +   theme_minimal()

title: Tidy Radiocarbon Data
text: c14 provides basic classes functions radiocarbon data R. makes easier combine methods several existing packages (e.g. rcarbon, Bchron, oxcAAR, c14bazAAR, ArchaeoPhases, stratigraphr) together work tidy data workflow. forked stratigraphr v0.3.0.

title: Tidy Radiocarbon Data
text: can install development version c14 GitHub remotes package: Note dependency controller also yet available CRAN.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github(c("joeroe/controller", "joeroe/c14"))

title: Tidy Radiocarbon Data
text: main aim c14 make easier work radiocarbon data within tidy workflow. example, can combine dplyr::filter() c14_calibrate() calibrate dates specific site: resulting cal-class vector can assigned new column, allowing us keep working data context original data frame tibble.
code: library("c14") library("dplyr", warn.conflicts = FALSE)  ppnd |>   filter(site == "Ganj Dareh") |>   select(lab_id, cra, error) |>   mutate(cal = c14_calibrate(cra, error)) #> # A tibble: 9 × 4 #>   lab_id     cra error                cal #>   <chr>    <int> <int>              <cal> #> 1 GaK 807  10400   150 11755–12710 cal BP #> 2 OxA 2099  8840   110  9560–10190 cal BP #> 3 OxA 2100  9010   110  9750–10485 cal BP #> 4 OxA 2101  8850   100  9560–10195 cal BP #> 5 OxA 2102  8690   110  9490–10145 cal BP #> 6 SI 922    8570   210  9035–10180 cal BP #> 7 SI 923    8625   195  9145–10200 cal BP #> 8 SI 924    8640    90  9475–10105 cal BP #> 9 SI 925    8385    75   9145–9530 cal BP

what: Convert a foreign object to a cal_dist object — as_cal_dist
title: Convert a foreign object to a cal_dist object — as_cal_dist
text: as_cal_dist() converts objects packages represent calibrated radiocarbon distributions c14 cal_dist class. Methods currently implemented : CalDates: rcarbon::calibrate() oxcAARCalibratedDate oxcAARCalibratedDatesList: oxcAAR::oxcalCalibrate() BchronCalibratedDates: Bchron::BchronCalibrate() functions intended complex S3 objects packages. generic constructor cal_dist() can used data frames base structures.

title: Convert a foreign object to a cal_dist object — as_cal_dist
code: as_cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' as_cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' as_cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'CalDates' as_cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'oxcAARCalibratedDatesList' as_cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'oxcAARCalibratedDate' as_cal_dist(x, ..., which = c("prior", "posterior"))  # S3 method for class 'BchronCalibratedDates' as_cal_dist(x, ...)

title: Convert a foreign object to a cal_dist object — as_cal_dist
text: x Object another package converted cal_dist object. ... Unused probability distribution extract oxcAARCalibratedDate: "prior" (default) uses raw calibrated probabilities. "posterior" uses Bayesian posterior probabilities (error available).

title: Convert a foreign object to a cal_dist object — as_cal_dist
text: Vector class c14_cal_dist (cal_dist).

Calibrate radiocarbon dates — c14_calibrate
text: Transforms 'raw' radiocarbon ages calendar probability distribution using calibration curve.

title: Calibrate radiocarbon dates — c14_calibrate
code: c14_calibrate(c14_age, c14_error, c14_curve = IntCal20)

title: Calibrate radiocarbon dates — c14_calibrate
text: c14_age Vector uncalibrated radiocarbon ages. c14_error Vector standard errors associated c14_age. c14_curve c14_curve object list c14_curve objects. See c14_curves list standard curves provided packages. Default: IntCal20.

title: Calibrate radiocarbon dates — c14_calibrate
text: cal vector.

title: Control radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_control_lab_id
text: Standardises vector radiocarbon laboratory identifiers common format. Specifically, c14_control_lab_id(): Fixes malformed identifiers c14_fix_lab_id() Parses lab code lab number components (c14_parse_lab_id()) Standardises lab codes thesaurus, default c14_lab_code_thesaurus Reunites lab codes numbers uniform seperator (default: "-")

title: Control radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_control_lab_id
code: c14_control_lab_id(   x,   thesaurus = c14_lab_code_thesaurus,   sep = "-",   quiet = FALSE,   warn_unmatched = TRUE )

title: Control radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_control_lab_id
text: x Vector radiocarbon laboratory identifiers. thesaurus Thesaurus use lab codes. Defaults c14_lab_code_thesaurus thesaraus included package. sep Character use seperate lab codes numbers result. Default: "-". quiet Passed controller::control(). Set TRUE suppress messages replaced values. Default: FALSE. warn_unmatched Passed controller::control(). Set FALSE suppress warnings unmatched values. Default: TRUE.

title: Control radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_control_lab_id
text: Vector length x controlled laboratory identifiers.

title: Control radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_control_lab_id
code: c14_control_lab_id(c("OxA-1234", "OxA 1234", "Oxa 1234")) #> Replaced values: #> ℹ Oxa → OxA #> [1] "OxA-1234" "OxA-1234" "OxA-1234"

title: Control radiocarbon sample materials — c14_control_material

title: Control radiocarbon sample materials — c14_control_material
code: c14_control_material(   x,   thesaurus = c14_material_thesaurus,   quiet = FALSE,   warn_unmatched = TRUE )

title: Control radiocarbon sample materials — c14_control_material
text: x Vector radiocarbon sample materials. thesaurus Thesaurus use sample materials. Defaults c14_material_thesaurus thesaraus included package. quiet Passed controller::control(). Set TRUE suppress messages replaced values. Default: FALSE. warn_unmatched Passed controller::control(). Set FALSE suppress warnings unmatched values. Default: TRUE.

title: Control radiocarbon sample materials — c14_control_material
text: Vector length x controlled sample materials.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curve class — c14_curve
text: c14_curve class represents radiocarbon calibration curve, estimates calendar ages corresponding set radiocarbon measurements. Usually calibration performed either one standard curves included package (see c14_curves) read file read_14c(). c14_curve() constructs new calibration curve using values 14C years, optionally associated d14C values. c14_fcurve() constructs curve using fraction modern values (F14C pMC), typically used "post-bomb" calibration.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curve class — c14_curve
code: c14_curve(   cal_age = era::yr(),   c14_age = numeric(),   c14_error = numeric(),   d14c = numeric(),   d14c_error = numeric() )  c14_fcurve(cal_age = era::yr(), f14c = numeric(), f14c_error = numeric())

title: Radiocarbon calibration curve class — c14_curve
text: cal_age Vector calendar ages. cal_age object class era::yr, epoch respected, otherwise assumed "cal BP". c14_age Vector uncalibrated radiocarbon ages. c14_error Vector errors (sigma values) associated c14_age. d14c Vector Delta-14C values (optional). d14c_error Vector errors (sigma values) associated d14c (optional). f14c Vector fraction modern (F14C pMC) values. f14c_error Vector errors (sigma values) associated f14c.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curve class — c14_curve
text: Object class c14_curve.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curve class — c14_curve
text: Stenström KE, Skog G, Georgiadou E, Genberg J, Johansson (2011). “guide radiocarbon units calculations.” Technical Report LUNFD6(NFFR-3111)/1-17/(2011), Lund University, Nuclear Physics.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curves — c14_curves
text: Radiocarbon calibration requires calibration curve: estimate true calendar age associated radiocarbon measurement. following curves included c14 package: IntCal20 IntCal Northern Hemisphere curve, 0–55,000 cal BP (Reimer et al. 2020) . SHCal20 IntCal Southern Hemisphere curve, 0–55,000 cal BP (Hogg et al. 2020) . Marine20 IntCal marine curve, 0–55,000 cal BP (Heaton et al. 2020) . IntCal13, IntCal09, IntCal04, IntCal98 Previous IntCal Northern Hemisphere curves (Reimer et al. 2013; Reimer et al. 2009; Reimer et al. 2004; Stuiver et al. 1998) . Superseded IntCal20. SHCal13, SHCal04 Previous IntCal Southern Hemisphere curves (Hogg et al. 2013; McCormac et al. 2004) . Superseded SHCal20. Marine13, Marine09, Marine04, Marine98 Previous IntCal marine curves (Reimer et al. 2013; Reimer et al. 2009; Hughen et al. 2004; Stuiver et al. 1998) . Superseded Marine20. Generally, recommended use latest curve IntCal series: IntCal20 samples northern hemisphere; SHCal20 samples southern hemisphere; Marine20 samples obtained 14C marine environment.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curves — c14_curves
code: IntCal20  SHCal20  Marine20  IntCal13  SHCal13  Marine13  IntCal09  Marine09  IntCal04  SHCal04  Marine04  IntCal98  Marine98

title: Radiocarbon calibration curves — c14_curves
text: c14_curve object following fields. standard curves: cal_age Calendar ages associated era (era::yr class). c14_age Uncalibrated radiocarbon ages. c14_error Errors (sigma values) associated c14_age. d14c Delta-14C values. d14c_error Errors (sigma values) associated d14c. post-bomb curves: cal_age Calendar ages associated era (era::yr class). f14c Fraction modern (F14C pMC) values. f14c_error Errors (sigma values) associated f14c. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 9501 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 9501 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 5501 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 5141 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 5141 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 4801 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 3522 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 3651 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 3302 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 2482 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 3301 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 1538 rows 5 columns. object class c14_curve_14c (inherits c14_curve, data.frame) 1569 rows 5 columns.

title: Radiocarbon calibration curves — c14_curves
text: http://intcal.org/curves/ http://calib.org/CALIBomb/

title: Radiocarbon calibration curves — c14_curves
text: Heaton TJ, Köhler P, Butzin M, Bard E, Reimer RW, Austin WEN, Ramsey CB, Grootes PM, Hughen KA, Kromer B, Reimer PJ, Adkins J, Burke , Cook MS, Olsen J, Skinner LC (2020). “Marine20—Marine Radiocarbon Age Calibration Curve (0–55,000 cal BP).” Radiocarbon, 62(4), 779–820. ISSN 0033-8222, 1945-5755. doi:10.1017/RDC.2020.68 . Hogg AG, Heaton TJ, Hua Q, Palmer JG, Turney CSM, Southon J, Bayliss , Blackwell PG, Boswijk G, Ramsey CB, Pearson C, Petchey F, Reimer P, Reimer R, Wacker L (2020). “SHCal20 Southern Hemisphere Calibration, 0–55,000 Years cal BP.” Radiocarbon, 62(4), 759–778. ISSN 0033-8222, 1945-5755. doi:10.1017/RDC.2020.59 . title: Radiocarbon decay constants — c14_decay
text: MathJax = {output: {font: "mathjax-newcm", fontPath: "../../mathjaxr/doc/mathjax/font",}};  Decay constants used calculation radiocarbon ages (see c14_age()).

title: Radiocarbon decay constants — c14_decay
code: c14_decay_libby  c14_decay_cambridge

title: Radiocarbon decay constants — c14_decay
text: numeric length 1: c14_decay_libby Libby decay constant, \(\lambda_L=8033^{-1}\). standard calculating conventional radiocarbon age (CRA). c14_decay_cambridge Cambridge decay constant, \(\lambda_C=8267^{-1}\). object class numeric length 1. object class numeric length 1.

title: Radiocarbon decay constants — c14_decay
text: Stenström KE, Skog G, Georgiadou E, Genberg J, Johansson (2011). “guide radiocarbon units calculations.” Technical Report LUNFD6(NFFR-3111)/1-17/(2011), Lund University, Nuclear Physics.

what: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
text: c14_is_lab_id() tests whether lab ID matches conventional format, e.g. "OxA-1234" "OxA 5678". c14_fix_lab_id() attempts coerce malformed lab IDs format, fixing common issues variant forms.

title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
code: c14_fix_lab_id(x)  c14_is_lab_id(x)

title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
text: x Vector potentially malformed lab IDs.

title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
text: c14_is_lab_id() returns logical vector length x. c14_fix_lab_id() returns x values replaced match conventional format possible.

title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
text: regular expression used test conventional format "^([[:alpha:]\(\)/]{1,8})[ -\u2010\u2013_#\.\+](.*)$". accepts unusual parsable variants "Ki(KIEV)-1234" "Gif/LSN_5678"

title: Detect and fix malformed radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_fix_lab_id
code: # Valid formats c14_is_lab_id(c("OxA-1234", "OxA 1234", "OxA", "Gif/LSN_5678")) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE  # Invalid formats c14_is_lab_id(c("1234", "1234-OxA")) #> [1] FALSE FALSE  # Fix c14_fix_lab_id(c("OxA", "Gif/LSN_5678", "Ki(KIEV)-1234")) #> [1] "OxA"           "Gif/LSN_5678"  "Ki(KIEV)-1234"

title: Radiocarbon laboratory code thesaurus — c14_lab_code_thesaurus
text: thesaurus canonical (matching given c14_labs) radiocarbon laboratory codes variants codes observed various published datasets.

title: Radiocarbon laboratory code thesaurus — c14_lab_code_thesaurus
code: c14_lab_code_thesaurus

title: Radiocarbon laboratory code thesaurus — c14_lab_code_thesaurus
text: data frame 688 rows 2 variables: canon Character. preferred code laboratory. variant Character. Variant codes.

title: Radiocarbon laboratory code thesaurus — c14_lab_code_thesaurus
text: thesaurus aims precise conservative. , canonical forms given variants can unambiguously interpreted known laboratory listed c14_labs. case sensitive, laboratory codes can distinguished case (see c14_labs examples). Variants (yet) unambiguously matched known, standard lab code canon value NA.

title: Radiocarbon laboratories — c14_labs
text: Table active defunct radiocarbon laboratories standard lab codes. Primarily based list maintained journal Radiocarbon (updated 20 July 2021), additions.

title: Radiocarbon laboratories — c14_labs
code: c14_labs

title: Radiocarbon laboratories — c14_labs
text: data frame 297 rows 4 variables: lab_code Character. Laboratory code, used identify published dates lab. active Logical. FALSE laboratory "closed, longer measuring 14C, operating different code". lab Character. Name laboratory. country Character.

title: Radiocarbon laboratories — c14_labs
text: lab_code almost unique, exceptions, sometimes case distinguishes two labs: "Gd" (Gliwice) "GD" (Gdansk, defunct); "KI" (Kiel, now "KiA") "Ki" (Kiev, now "Ki(KIEV)"); "Lu" (Lund, now "LuS", formerly "LuA") "LU" (St. Petersburg); "P" (Max Planck) "P" (Pennsylvania, defunct); "Pi" (Pisa, defunct) "PI" (Permafrost Institute, defunct); "TKA" (Univ. Tokyo Museum) "TKa" (Univ. Tokyo AMS), also "TK" (Univ. Tokyo).

title: Radiocarbon laboratories — c14_labs
text: List known radiocarbon laboratories. Radiocarbon. Last updated 20 July 2021. http://radiocarbon.webhost.uits.arizona.edu/node/11 Wang, C., Lu, H., Zhang, J., Gu, Z. , K., 2014. Prehistoric demographic fluctuations China inferred radiocarbon data linkage climate change past 50,000 years. Quaternary Science Reviews, 98, pp.45-59. doi:10.1016/j.quascirev.2014.05.015

title: Radiocarbon sample material thesaurus — c14_material_thesaurus
text: thesaurus standardised radiocarbon sample material descriptions variants materials observed various published datasets.

title: Radiocarbon sample material thesaurus — c14_material_thesaurus
code: c14_material_thesaurus

title: Radiocarbon sample material thesaurus — c14_material_thesaurus
text: data frame 8854 rows 2 variables: canon Character. preferred description sample material. variant Character. Variant descriptions.

title: Radiocarbon sample material thesaurus — c14_material_thesaurus
text: Originally compiled Clemens Schmid, David Matzig, Dirk Seidensticker, Joe Roe, Thomas Huet, package c14bazAAR.

title: Parse radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_parse_lab_id
text: Splits vector radiocarbon lab IDs (e.g. "OxA-1234") constituent parts: lab code ("OxA") quasi-numeric unique identifier ("1324"). x contains values fit standard format ("OxA-1234" "OxA 4567"), default function attempt fix c14_fix_lab_id() parsing.

title: Parse radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_parse_lab_id
code: c14_parse_lab_id(x, fix = TRUE)

title: Parse radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_parse_lab_id
text: x Vector lab IDs, e.g. "OxA-1234". fix TRUE (default), attempts fix malformed lab IDs parsing.

title: Parse radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_parse_lab_id
text: data frame two character columns: lab_code lab_number. x length one, simplified named character vector length 2.

title: Parse radiocarbon laboratory identifiers — c14_parse_lab_id
code: c14_parse_lab_id(c("OxA-1234", "Ly 456", "RT 1234-5678")) #>   lab_code lab_number #> 1      OxA       1234 #> 2       Ly        456 #> 3       RT  1234-5678  # By default, tries to fix malformed values of x before parsing # Use fix = FALSE for strict parsing c14_parse_lab_id(c("OxA 1234", "OxA_5678", "Gif/LSN-123")) #>   lab_code lab_number #> 1      OxA       1234 #> 2      OxA       5678 #> 3  Gif/LSN        123 c14_parse_lab_id(c("OxA 1234", "OxA_5678", "Gif/LSN-123"), fix = FALSE) #>   lab_code lab_number #> 1      OxA       1234 #> 2      OxA       5678 #> 3  Gif/LSN        123  # Single values of x are simplified to a character vector c14_parse_lab_id("OxA 1234") #>   lab_code lab_number  #>      "OxA"     "1234"

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
text: MathJax = {output: {font: "mathjax-newcm", fontPath: "../../mathjaxr/doc/mathjax/font",}};  Functions calculating basic units used radiocarbon measurements. c14_age() calculates conventional radiocarbon age (CRA) fraction modern measurement. c14_f14c() reverse-calculates fractionation-corrected fraction modern value (\(F^{14}C\) \(pM\)) radiocarbon age.

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
code: c14_age(x, decay = c14::c14_decay_libby)  c14_f14c(x, decay = c14::c14_decay_libby)

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
text: x c14_age(), vector fraction modern (\(F^{14}C\)) measurements. c14_f14c(), vector conventional radiocarbon ages. decay Decay constant. default Libby constant (c14_decay_libby), standard calculating conventional radiocarbon ages. Use c14_decay_cambridge Cambridge constant, single numeric values.

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
text: Vector length x.

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
text: c14_age() calculates conventional radiocarbon age, \(t\), defined Stuiver Polach (1977) : \[t = -\frac{1}{\lambda}\ln{F^{14}C}\] c14_f14c() implements inverse function: \[F^{14}C = e^{-\lambda t}\] decay constant conventionally used calculating radiocarbon ages Libby decay constant, \(\lambda_L=8033^{-1}\). alternative Cambridge decay constant, \(\lambda_C=8267^{-1}\) (Stenström et al. 2011) . Reported radiocarbon ages usually rounded based magnitude error (Stuiver Polach 1977) . reason, reverse-calculating fraction modern radiocarbon age unlikely return precise original measurement sample. available, fraction modern recommended measurement calibration (Bronk Ramsey 2008) .

title: Radiocarbon unit calculations — c14_units
text: Bronk Ramsey C (2008). “Radiocarbon dating: revolutions understanding.” Archaeometry, 50(2), 249–275. ISSN 0003-813X, 1475-4754. doi:10.1111/j.1475-4754.2008.00394.x . Stenström KE, Skog G, Georgiadou E, Genberg J, Johansson (2011). “guide radiocarbon units calculations.” Technical Report LUNFD6(NFFR-3111)/1-17/(2011), Lund University, Nuclear Physics. Stuiver M, Polach HA (1977). “Reporting 14C Data.” Radiocarbon, 19(3), 355–363. ISSN 0033-8222, 1945-5755. doi:10.1017/S0033822200003672 .

title: Calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal
text: cal class represents vector calibrated radiocarbon dates lazy record. element stores parameters needed derive calendar probability distribution (c14_age, c14_error, c14_curve), compute distribution requested.

title: Calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal
code: cal(c14_age = double(), c14_error = double(), curve = c14_curve())

title: Calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal
text: c14_age Vector uncalibrated radiocarbon ages. c14_error Vector standard errors associated c14_age. curve c14_curve object list c14_curve objects. Default: IntCal20.

title: Calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal
text: vector class cal (c14_cal).

what: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
text: Aggregates calibrated radiocarbon dates calendar probability distributions using composite kernel density estimate (cKDE) method method (Brown 2017) . involves estimating density set dates repeatedly computing kernel density estimate random sample ages drawn probability distributions.

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
code: cal_density(x, bw = 30, ..., times = 25, bootstrap = TRUE, strata = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'c14_cal' density(x, ...)

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
text: x cal vector calendar probability distributions. bw Kernel bandwidth size passed stats::density(). Can either integer value character selection rule, latter likely result different bandwidth applied bootstrapped sample therefore avoided. Default: 30 (years). ... arguments passed stats::density(). times Number bootstrap samples generate. default 25 suitable testing set much higher practice. bootstrap TRUE (default), randomly resamples replacement x KDE calculation. strata NULL, KDEs weighted ensure equal representation classes specified factor character vector. bootstrap = TRUE, bootstrap samples generated using stratified resampling classes.

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
text: result data frame tibble::tibble three columns: age (interpolated common range x), .estimate (mean bootstrapped KDEs), .error (standard error bootstrapped KDEs).

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
text: cal_density(..., bootstrap = TRUE) performs composite kernel density estimation bootstrapping (McLaughlin 2019) , sampling error. x estimated randomly resampling x KDE calculation. See McLaughlin (2019)  Crema (2022)  discussions bandwith selection.

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
text: Brown WA (2017). “Past Future Growth Rate Estimation Demographic Temporal Frequency Analysis: Biodemographic Interpretability Ascendance Dynamic Growth Models.” Journal Archaeological Science, 80, 96–108. ISSN 0305-4403. doi:10.1016/j.jas.2017.02.003 . Crema ER (2022). “Statistical Inference Prehistoric Demography Frequency Distributions Radiocarbon Dates: Review Guide Perplexed.” Journal Archaeological Method Theory, 29(4), 1387–1418. ISSN 1573-7764. doi:10.1007/s10816-022-09559-5 . McLaughlin TR (2019). “Applications Space–Time Modelling Open-Source 14C Age Calibration.” Journal Archaeological Method Theory, 26(2), 479–501. ISSN 1573-7764. doi:10.1007/s10816-018-9381-3 .

title: Kernel density estimation of calendar probability distributions — cal_density
code: data(shub1_c14) shub1_cal <- c14_calibrate(shub1_c14$c14_age, shub1_c14$c14_error) cal_density(shub1_cal) #> # A tibble: 14,792 × 3 #>           age .estimate   .error #>          <yr>     <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 789 cal BP  2.83e-14 1.41e-13 #>  2 790 cal BP  1.90e-13 9.50e-13 #>  3 791 cal BP  3.52e-13 1.76e-12 #>  4 792 cal BP  5.14e-13 2.57e-12 #>  5 793 cal BP  6.75e-13 3.38e-12 #>  6 794 cal BP  8.37e-13 4.18e-12 #>  7 795 cal BP  9.99e-13 4.99e-12 #>  8 796 cal BP  1.16e-12 5.80e-12 #>  9 797 cal BP  1.32e-12 6.61e-12 #> 10 798 cal BP  1.48e-12 7.42e-12 #> # ℹ 14,782 more rows  # Stratify and weight bootstrap estimation by phase cal_density(shub1_cal, strata = shub1_c14$phase) #> # A tibble: 14,792 × 3 #>           age .estimate   .error #>          <yr>     <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1 789 cal BP  4.09e-14 1.79e-13 #>  2 790 cal BP  2.76e-13 1.20e-12 #>  3 791 cal BP  5.12e-13 2.23e-12 #>  4 792 cal BP  7.47e-13 3.25e-12 #>  5 793 cal BP  9.83e-13 4.28e-12 #>  6 794 cal BP  1.22e-12 5.30e-12 #>  7 795 cal BP  1.45e-12 6.33e-12 #>  8 796 cal BP  1.69e-12 7.35e-12 #>  9 797 cal BP  1.92e-12 8.38e-12 #> 10 798 cal BP  2.16e-12 9.40e-12 #> # ℹ 14,782 more rows

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
text: cal_dist class represents vector calendar probability distributions. cal_dist() S3 generic constructs new cal_dist vector: cal object: evaluates calibrated probability distribution data.frame: constructs raw age probability density data

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
code: cal_dist(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'c14_cal' cal_dist(x, at = NULL, ...)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' cal_dist(x, ..., .era = era::era("cal BP"))  # Default S3 method cal_dist(x, ...)

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
text: x object convert cal_dist. Currently supports cal data.frame objects. ... Additional arguments passed methods. List numeric vectors giving ages distribution calculated. Recycled length(cal). NULL (default) plausible range estimated calibrated date native resolution calibration curve (see details). .era era::era() object describing time scale used ages. Defaults calendar years Present (era("cal BP")). used data.frame method ages already era::yr() vectors.

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
text: list data frames class cal_dist (c14_cal_dist). element two columns: age pdens.

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
text: 'plausible range' calibrated radiocarbon distribution estimated portions calibration curve six times combined error determination curve away conventional radiocarbon age.

title: Calendar probability distribution — cal_dist
code: # From data frames: cal_dist(data.frame(age = era::yr(1:10, "cal BP"), pdens = rep(0.1, 10))) #> <c14_cal_dist[1]> #> [1] c. 1 cal BP  # From cal objects: cal <- c14::cal(5000, 10, c14::IntCal20) cal_dist(cal) #> <c14_cal_dist[1]> #> [1] c. 5730 cal BP

what: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
title: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
text: cal_hdr() calculates Highest Density Region (HDR) calibrated radiocarbon dates. HDR shortest interval (set intervals) contains specified proportion probability mass. cal_hdi() calculates Highest Density Interval (HDI), single shortest contiguous interval containing specified proportion probability mass. Unlike cal_hdr(), can return multiple intervals multimodal distributions, cal_hdi() always returns exactly one interval.

title: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
code: cal_hdr(x, interval = 0.954)  cal_hdi(x, interval = 0.954)

title: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
text: x cal vector calibrated radiocarbon dates. interval Numeric. probability content HDR/HDI. Default: 0.954 (approximately 2 standard deviations).

title: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
text: cal_hdr(): list length x. element list numeric vectors, vector two elements: start end ages HDR interval. multimodal distributions, multiple intervals may returned. cal_hdi(): list length x. element numeric vector two elements: start end ages HDI interval.

title: Highest Density Region and Highest Density Interval — cal_hdr
code: x <- c14_calibrate(5000, 10) cal_hdr(x) #> [[1]] #> [[1]][[1]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5657 5749 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #> [[1]][[2]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5828 5849 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #>  cal_hdi(x) #> [[1]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5657 5849 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  cal_hdr(x, interval = 0.683) #> [[1]] #> [[1]][[1]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5663 5686 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #> [[1]][[2]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5712 5744 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>  #>  cal_hdi(x, interval = 0.683) #> [[1]] #> # cal BP years <yr[2]>: #> [1] 5663 5744 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 #>

what: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
text: Functions deriving single point estimate calendar age calibrated radiocarbon dates. Note none methods produce good estimate (Michczyński 2007) , used last resort probability distribution function interval estimate appropriate, case cal_mode() (default) recommended.

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
code: cal_mode(x, quiet = FALSE)  cal_median(x)  cal_mean(x)  cal_local_mode(x, interval = 0.954)  cal_central(x, interval = 0.954)

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
text: x cal object. vector calibrated radiocarbon dates. quiet Set quiet = TRUE suppress warnings (used cal_mode()). interval Numeric. confidence interval cal_local_mode() cal_central(). Default: 0.954.

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
text: Numeric vector length x giving point estimate calibrated date.

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
text: cal_mode() Age corresponding maximum peak probability distribution. x one modal value, first returned. cal_median() Age corresponding median quantile probability distribution. cal_mean() Mean age weighted probability density. cal_local_mode() Age corresponding maximum peak probability distribution within confidence range specified interval (yet implemented). cal_central() Age corresponding central point probability density within confidence range specified interval (yet implemented).

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
text: Michczyński (2007). “Possible Find Good Point Estimate Calibrated Radiocarbon Date?” Radiocarbon, 49(2), 393–401. ISSN 0033-8222, 1945-5755. doi:10.1017/S0033822200042326 .

title: Point estimates of calibrated radiocarbon dates — cal_mode
code: x <- c14_calibrate(10000, 30)  cal_mode(x) #> # cal BP years <yr[1]>: #> [1] 11400 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 cal_median(x) #> # cal BP years <yr[1]>: #> [1] 11475 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950 cal_mean(x) #> # cal BP years <yr[1]>: #> [1] 11479.82 #> # Era: Before Present (cal BP): Gregorian years (365.2425 days), counted backwards from 1950

what: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
text: Calculates probability calibrated radiocarbon date falls within given calendar age range single year.

title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
code: cal_probability(x, from, to = NULL)

title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
text: Rundkvist, Martin. 20 November 2024. Radiocarbon AD 900s Riddarholmen. Aardvarchaeology. https://aardvarchaeology.wordpress.com/2024/11/20/radiocarbon---ad-900s--riddarholmen/

title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
text: x cal vector calibrated radiocarbon dates. Numeric. Start age range (inclusive), era calibration (typically cal BP). Numeric NULL. End age range (inclusive). NULL (default), returns probability mass single year given .

title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
text: Numeric vector probabilities 0 1, length x.

title: Probability that a calibrated date falls within an age range — cal_probability
code: x <- c14_calibrate(1116, 30)  # Probability within an age range cal_probability(x, 970, 1060) #> [1] 0.8086552  # Probability at a single year cal_probability(x, 5000) #> [1] 0

title: Sum calendar probability distributions — cal_sum
text: Aggregates vector calendar probability distributions (calibrated radiocarbon dates) summing given range ages.

title: Sum calendar probability distributions — cal_sum
code: cal_sum(x, range = NULL, normalise = FALSE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'c14_cal' sum(x, ..., normalise = FALSE)

title: Sum calendar probability distributions — cal_sum
text: x cal vector calendar probability distributions. range Range ages sum. Defaults maximum range x native resolution. normalise Whether normalise summed distribution sum 1. default normalise. ... used

title: Sum calendar probability distributions — cal_sum
text: summed probability distribution cal_dist vector length 1.

title: Sum calendar probability distributions — cal_sum
code: shub1_cal <- c14_calibrate(shub1_c14$c14_age, shub1_c14$c14_error) cal_sum(shub1_cal) #> <c14_cal_dist[1]> #> [1] c. 14250 cal BP

title: Plot calibrated radiocarbon dates — plot.c14_cal
text: Plots element cal vector separate calendar probability distribution. Use par(mfrow) par(mfcol) arrange multiple plots.

title: Plot calibrated radiocarbon dates — plot.c14_cal
code: # S3 method for class 'c14_cal' plot(   x,   ...,   max.plot = 20,   resolution = NULL,   fixed_xlim = FALSE,   show_curve = TRUE )

title: Plot calibrated radiocarbon dates — plot.c14_cal
text: x cal vector calibrated radiocarbon dates. ... Additional arguments passed plot.default(). max.plot Maximum number plots generate. warning issued length(x) > max.plot first max.plot elements plotted. Default: 20. resolution Resolution age grid years. NULL (default), automatically calculated produce approximately 500 points across age range smooth curves. fixed_xlim Logical. TRUE, plots share x-axis range (calculated dates). FALSE (default), plot uses age range focused specific date. show_curve Logical. TRUE (default), plot calibration curve main plot probability distribution overlaid right y-axis. FALSE, show probability distribution.

title: Plot calibrated radiocarbon dates — plot.c14_cal
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect generating plots.

title: Plot calibrated radiocarbon dates — plot.c14_cal
code: x <- cal(c(1000, 2000, 3000), c(30, 40, 50), IntCal20) plot(x)     # Arrange multiple plots par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) plot(x) dev.off() #> null device  #>           1   # Use same fixed x axis limits for all plots plot(x, fixed_xlim = TRUE)  # Hide calibration curve plot(x[1], show_curve = FALSE)

what: Radiocarbon dates from Neolithic Southwest Asia — ppnd
title: Radiocarbon dates from Neolithic Southwest Asia — ppnd
text: dataset 1049 radiocarbon dates Epipalaeolithic Neolithic sites Southwest Asia, compiled Marion Benz Platform Neolithic Radiocarbon Dates (PPND).

title: Radiocarbon dates from Neolithic Southwest Asia — ppnd
code: ppnd

title: Radiocarbon dates from Neolithic Southwest Asia — ppnd
text: data frame 1049 rows 10 variables: lab_id Character. Laboratory code ID dated sample. site Character. Name site sample retrieved. latitude, longitude Double. Coordinates site. context Character. Description context sample retrieved. cra Integer. Conventional radiocarbon age (CRA) sample Present. error Integer. Error associated radiocarbon measurement. d13c Character. d13C measurement error (separated plus/minus symbol) associated sample. material Character. Description sample material. references Character. Bibliographic reference associated sample. See bibliography https://www.exoriente.org/associated_projects/ppnd_references.php.

title: Radiocarbon dates from Neolithic Southwest Asia — ppnd
text: https://www.exoriente.org/associated_projects/ppnd.php (Retrieved 2018-04-02)

title: Read calibration curve files — read_14c
text: Reads .14c .f14c formats used radiocarbon calibration curves returns c14_curve object. files, first argument needs supplied. arguments can used file follow conventional format.

title: Read calibration curve files — read_14c
code: read_14c(   file,   format = c("14c", "f14c"),   delim = NA,   cal_era = "cal BP",   col_names = switch(format, `14c` = c("cal_age", "c14_age", "c14_error", "d14c",     "d14c_error"), f14c = c("cal_age", "f14c", "f14c_error")) )

title: Read calibration curve files — read_14c
text: file Path, connection, URL calibration curve file, typically extension .14c .f14c. format "14c" "f14c". supplied, inferred extension file. delim Character used separate fields. NA (default), inferred format: "," .14c files "" (whitespace) .f14c files. cal_era Character label era::era object describing time scale used calibration curve's cal_age field. Default: "cal BP". col_names Vector column names specifying order fields file. supplied, assumed usual order. Must contain following elements, depending value format: format = "14c": "cal_age", "c14_age", "c14_error", optionally "d14c" "d14c_error" format = "f14c": "cal_age", "f14c", "f14c_error" See c14_curve() details fields.

title: Read calibration curve files — read_14c
text: c14_curve object.

title: Read calibration curve files — read_14c
code: read_14c(system.file("extdata", "intcal04.14c", package = "c14")) #> # custom curve <c14_curve_14c> #> # Conventional radiocarbon age calibration curve #> # Range: -5–26000 cal BP #>   cal_age c14_age c14_error  d14c d14c_error #> 1   26000   21341       108 629.9       21.9 #> 2   25980   21348       103 624.5       20.8 #> 3   25960   21354        98 619.4       19.8 #> 4   25940   21360        93 614.3       18.7 #> 5   25920   21366        88 609.2       17.6 #> # 3297 more rows

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
text: Radiocarbon dates Shubayqa 1, Epipalaeolithic site eastern Jordan, Richter et al. (2017) .

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
code: shub1_c14

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
text: data frame 27 rows 8 variables: lab_id character; standardised lab code uniquely identifying dated sample. context integer; schematic context number sample found (see details). phase character; phase sample assigned . sample character; description sample context, including real context number. material character; description sample material. c14_age integer; conventional radiocarbon age sample years cal BP. c14_error integer; standard error associated radiocarbon measurement ± years cal BP. outlier logical; whether sample considered outlier.

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
text: Richter et al. (2017)

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
text: context refers schematic stratigraphy included stratigraphr::shub1; real context number described sample.

title: Radiocarbon dates from Shubayqa 1 — shub1_c14
text: Richter T, Arranz-Otaegui , Yeomans L, Boaretto E (2017). “High Resolution AMS Dates Shubayqa 1, northeast Jordan Reveal Complex Origins Late Epipalaeolithic Natufian Levant.” Scientific reports, 7(1), 17025. ISSN 2045-2322. doi:10.1038/s41598-017-17096-5 .

title: c14 v0.1.0.9000 (development version)
text: as_cal() renamed as_cal_dist() (#56) New introductory vignette package (vignette("c14")) New individual functions point summaries calibrated radiocarbon dates, replacing cal_point() (documented ?cal_point): cal_mode(), cal_median(), cal_mean(), cal_local_mode(), cal_central(). Package longer depends checkmate, curl (depends), mockery, readr, readxl, usethis (suggests), dplyr (suggests, still required build website) New cal_probability() summary probability calibrated date lies specified range (#38) New default plot() method cal vectors (#32) Default printing cal vectors now highest density interval Replaces cal_age_range(), cal_age_min(), cal_age_max() c14_calibrate() longer engine min_pdens arguments (#24, #26) c14_calibrate() now accepts curve argument (#9)

title: c14 v0.1.0
text: Development version December 2024. Major changes since fork stratigraphr: cal now vctrs::list_of subclass; list two-column data frames representing probability distribution age column cal element now era::yr vector. “Metadata” removed class definition cal_point() point estimates cal_age_range(), cal_age_min(), cal_age_max() simple ranges cal_sum() summed probability cal_density() composite kernel density estimation c14_curve vctrs record class representing calibration curve, subclasses c14_curve_14c c14_curve_f14c CRA-based F14C-based records respectively. c14_curve() constructs c14_curve_14c object c14_fcurve() constructs c14_curve_f14c object read_14c() reads .14c .f14c files ?c14_curves: exported datasets IntCal20, SHCal20, Marine20, IntCal13, SHCal13, Marine13, IntCal09, Marine09, IntCal04, SHCal04, Marine04, IntCal98, Marine98 curves. c14_age() calculating radiocarbon age fraction modern. c14_f14c() reverse-calculating fraction modern radiocarbon age. Decay constants c14_decay_libby c14_decay_cambridge. Added example datasets ppnd shub1_c14

title: c14 v0.0.0
text: initial version package forked stratigraphr v0.3.0
